_comment: Measured on the M1 (single core, 60 s). Community record engines on their NATIVE instances — they cannot take the study's corner-pinned variants. See community.md. Reported in a panel separate from the from-scratch leaderboard.

engines.name: mcgavin-c
engines.note: Strict backtracker; comparable to our strict wall (~208) on depth, ~3x our node throughput. Optimises for depth, not matched-edge score.
engines.collapse: Pinning our 3 corners collapses its fixed scan path 203 -> 21 (confirmed on multiple variants): the path never visits the corners early, so a pinned corner dead-ends it at once. This is why a fixed-scan-path engine can't take arbitrary pins.

engines.name: blackwood-cs
engines.note: Break-DFS whose record (470) was set on the 1-clue variant; its heuristic phase thrashes on the pinned 5-clue, reaching only ~34 of 256 cells in 60 s. Adapted to run (net8.0 retarget, one thread) — the search is unchanged.
